source_name: Defence alliances and strategic posture compiled profiles
notes: Manual compiled Defence, Alliances and Strategic Posture profile. Budget, workforce, capability, uncrewed-systems, alliance and industry rows are copied or summarised from named public source envelopes. Readiness-sensitive values stay unavailable.
source_rights: Project metadata/prose CC BY 4.0; upstream source rights remain with each publisher.
citation: Fuel Resilience AU defence alliances methodology; Department of Defence; DFAT; Office of National Intelligence; Australian Submarine Agency.

fields.method: Budget, workforce, force structure, public asset/acquisition rows, alliances/frameworks, industry context and readiness boundaries are separate fields. Asset counts are not readiness or operational availability.

takeaways.title: The loaded current budget row is A$63.4b for 2026-27 Defence funding
takeaways.text: The 2026 National Defence Strategy budget factsheet reports A$63.4b Defence funding for 2026-27 and around A$425b in capability investment over the decade to 2035-36.

takeaways.title: Public asset counts are not readiness
takeaways.text: The page loads selected public Navy, Air Force and Army capability rows, but it does not infer mission-capable rates, crewed availability, stockpile sufficiency or combat readiness.

takeaways.title: Framework type matters
takeaways.text: ANZUS is a formal security treaty, AUKUS is a trilateral security and technology partnership, Five Eyes is an intelligence partnership, and the Quad is a diplomatic partnership. They are not collapsed into one generic alliance score.

takeaways.title: Uncrewed coverage is by category, not by total inventory
takeaways.text: The page shows in-service (Triton, Integrator), development (Ghost Bat, Ghost Shark), counter-drone (LAND 156) and sovereign-program (SDIP autonomous systems, DIDS) rows separately. There is no clean public drone-only Defence budget, so that value stays unavailable.

budget_metrics.notes: Funding includes Defence, ASD, ASA and ANNPSR as labelled in the factsheet.
The source says around A$425b; dashboard keeps the source wording.
Planned expenditure category, not a delivered capability count.
Sustainment planned expenditure category, not an availability measure.

budget_metrics.metric_name: Defence spending as share of GDP
budget_metrics.notes: Not computed here. Leave unavailable until a current official row or documented GDP denominator is loaded.

force_structure.name: Australian Defence Force
force_structure.summary: The ADF includes ADF Headquarters, Joint Capabilities Group, Joint Operations Command and the three services: Army, Air Force and Navy.
force_structure.notes: Structure row only; no readiness or deployable-strength estimate.

force_structure.name: Permanent ADF workforce achievement
force_structure.summary: 59,147 permanent ADF workforce achievement against a 58,850 2024-25 budgeted requirement.
force_structure.notes: Workforce achievement row only; not deployability or readiness.

force_structure.name: Strategy of denial and integrated focused force
force_structure.summary: The 2026 NDS builds on the strategy of denial and an integrated, focused force, with self-reliance, industrial resilience and partner coordination as public themes.
force_structure.notes: High-level public strategy summary; not a threat comparison or war-fighting prediction.

section_coverage.notes: Current 2026 NDS budget factsheet rows loaded for funding, capability investment and expenditure categories.
Public organisation and workforce rows loaded; readiness and deployable-strength fields unavailable.
Selected official public asset and acquisition rows loaded; not exhaustive and not readiness.

Selected official Defence project rows loaded across in-service, development, counter-drone and sovereign-program categories; complete ADF drone inventory and clean drone-only budget remain unavailable.
ANZUS, AUKUS, Five Eyes, Quad, AUSMIN and PNG treaty profiles loaded with framework type separation.
Official industrial priorities and program context loaded; sensitive supply-chain depth and stockpile adequacy unavailable.
No readiness, mission-capable, stockpile, classified basing or live operational posture metric is loaded.

methodology_caveats: Budget rows are money and planned expenditure; they are not capability availability.
Asset and acquisition rows are public counts or statuses; they are not mission-capable rates.
Formal treaties, intelligence partnerships, technology partnerships and diplomatic forums are separate framework types.
No classified, secret, live readiness or sensitive operational posture data is shown.
Unavailable fields stay unavailable until a named public source supports the exact metric.
